SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

umm.digiquilt.model
Class Patch

java.lang.Object
  umm.digiquilt.model.Patch

public class Patch
extends Object

A patch is one "square" of the main block. It contains 16 tiles. (see Shapes.java to see how they are laid out).

Version:
$Revision: 1.3 $
Author:
Main.BrianOhs and Main.AndyMitchell, last changed by $Author: mitchella $ on $Date: 2009-06-21 23:41:03 $

Field Summary
static int MAXTILES
          The maximum number of tiles possible in any Patch.

Patch()
          Default constructor for a patch.
Patch(Fabric[] fabrics)
          (Re)Create a patch with certain Fabrics.
 
Method Summary

          Boolean method to test equality of Patches

          Returns a string array of the fabrics that make up this Patch.

          Get the height of this Patch, in quadrants.

          Returns a clone of the patch

          Method to get the Fractional coverage of a fabric for this Patch.

          Create a clone of this Patch, with the tiles of this patch moved to be as close to the top left corner as possible without changing the actual appearance of the shape.
 Fabric getSolidColor()
          Assuming this patch has only one color (other than transparent), returns that one color.

          Getter for a specific tile of the patch.

          Get the width of this Patch, in quadrants.

          Creating hashcode since we are overriding equals()

          Method to place a patch on top of this one, and replace the tiles of this patch with all non-transparent tiles from the new patch

          Replace the first given fabric with the second in this Patch.
 void rotateCCW()
          Method for rotating a patch counter-clockwise
 void rotateCW()
          Method for rotating a patch clockwise
protected  void setTile(int index, Fabric tile)
          Method to set the tiles in a Patch to a FabricTile.
 void swapFabrics(Fabric one, Fabric two)
          Swap the two given fabrics in this Patch.

Field Detail

MAXTILES

public static final int MAXTILES
The maximum number of tiles possible in any Patch.

Constant Field Values
Constructor Detail

Patch

public Patch()
Default constructor for a patch.

Patch

public Patch(Fabric[] fabrics)
(Re)Create a patch with certain Fabrics.

Parameters:
fabrics - an array of fabric names
Method Detail

equals

public boolean equals(Object object)
Boolean method to test equality of Patches

Overrides:
equals in class Object
Returns:
result True if the patches are equal.

hashCode

public int hashCode()
Creating hashcode since we are overriding equals()

Overrides:
hashCode in class Object

getSolidColor

public Fabric getSolidColor()
Assuming this patch has only one color (other than transparent), returns that one color. If it has more than one color, or if its only color is transparent, returns Fabric.TRANSPARENT.

Returns:
the one Fabric covering this patch, or if there's more than one, Fabric.TRANSPARENT.

getSmallPatch

public Patch getSmallPatch()
Create a clone of this Patch, with the tiles of this patch moved to be as close to the top left corner as possible without changing the actual appearance of the shape. This is so that small shapes (for example, the half-rectangle and quarter-square in Shapes.java) can be picked up, put down in a different quadrant of a Patch, and picked up again, all while behaving the same way.

Returns:
a shifted copy of this Patch

getWidth

public int getWidth()
Get the width of this Patch, in quadrants. A Patch with width 0 is completely empty, a Patch with width 1 is a small shape with either the left or right half completely empty, and a Patch with width 2 is a full sized patch.

Returns:
the width, in quadrants.

getHeight

public int getHeight()
Get the height of this Patch, in quadrants. A Patch with height 0 is completely empty, a Patch with height 1 is a small shape with either the top or bottom half completely empty, and a Patch with height 2 is a full sized patch.

Returns:
the height, in quadrants.

rotateCW

public void rotateCW()
Method for rotating a patch clockwise

rotateCCW

public void rotateCCW()
Method for rotating a patch counter-clockwise

getFabricList

public String[] getFabricList()
Returns a string array of the fabrics that make up this Patch. Can be used to store a Patch in text form, and reconstructed by passing the same String[] into the constructor.

Returns:
a string array of this Patch's fabrics

getTile

public Fabric getTile(int index)
Getter for a specific tile of the patch.

Parameters:
index - the tile number to get
Returns:
the FabricTile at that location

setTile

protected void setTile(int index,
                       Fabric tile)
Method to set the tiles in a Patch to a FabricTile. This should only be used internally or for testing purposes, the "right" thing to do is to use the constructor.

Parameters:
index - numbered location of FabricTile in Patch.
tile - input tile which will be set to the index location in Patch.

mergePatch

public void mergePatch(Patch patch,
                       double xPercent,
                       double yPercent)
Method to place a patch on top of this one, and replace the tiles of this patch with all non-transparent tiles from the new patch

Parameters:
patch - The patch to be merged on top of this one
xPercent - Horizontal location of patch drop, from 0.0 to 1.0 (left and right edge, respectively)
yPercent - Vertical location 0.0 (top) to 1.0 (bottom)

getPatchClone

public Patch getPatchClone()
Returns a clone of the patch

Returns:
clone a clone of Patch

replaceFabrics

public void replaceFabrics(Fabric replaceThis,
                           Fabric withThis)
Replace the first given fabric with the second in this Patch.

Parameters:
replaceThis - the fabric to be replaced
withThis - the fabric used to replace

swapFabrics

public void swapFabrics(Fabric one,
                        Fabric two)
Swap the two given fabrics in this Patch.

Parameters:
one - the fabric to replace two and to be replaced by two
two - the fabric to replace one and to be replaced by one

getPatchCoverage

public int getPatchCoverage(Fabric fabric)
Method to get the Fractional coverage of a fabric for this Patch.

Parameters:
fabric -
Returns:
Fractional coverage of the fabric.
